fields.label: Provider
fields.description: The provider is where your JIRA instance is hosted. Select Jira Cloud if your Jira is being hosted on Atlassian Cloud. Select Jira Data Center / Jira Server if your Jira is being hosted elsewhere.

fields.label: URL
fields.description: The URL is the root URL for your Atlassian instance (example: company.atlassian.net)

fields.label: Authentication method
fields.description: Select the authentication method to use when connecting to Jira

fields.label: User email
fields.description: The email address for the user/service account

fields.label: API Token
fields.description: The API token is the token associated with the user. You can create one here.

fields.label: Scoped Token
fields.description: Toggle this switch on if API Token with scopes is used

fields.label: Jira App Cloud Id
fields.description: Jira App Cloud Id is required to be used with API Token with Scopes

overrides.description: Jira App Cloud Id is required for OAuth 2.0 authentication

fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID for the Jira service account

fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: The OAuth 2.0 Client Secret for the Jira service account

relationships.description: Basic Auth credentials (authMethod='basicAuth'): the Atlassian account email and its API token, sent as HTTP Basic auth (username:token). When jsonData.user is empty the backend instead sends the token as a Bearer token (pkg/plugin.go:253-264).
Scoped API token routing (Basic Auth): enabling scopedToken routes requests through https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/<cloudId>, so cloudId is required when scopedToken is on (pkg/plugin.go:221-226).

OAuth 2.0 client-credentials (authMethod='oauth2'): the service-account client ID and secret, exchanged for a token at https://auth.atlassian.com/oauth/token, plus the Cloud ID that selects the https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/<cloudId> gateway base URL (pkg/plugin.go:214-244).
Selecting OAuth 2.0 forces the provider to Jira Cloud and disables the Provider selector (src/components/ConfigEditor.tsx:94-96,200); OAuth 2.0 is Jira Cloud only.

instructions.msg: Two authentication methods are available, selected via jsonData.authMethod: 'basicAuth' (Basic Auth, the default) and 'oauth2' (OAuth 2.0 — Service Account). Configure exactly one; only the selected method's inputs are validated and used. When jsonData.authMethod is empty or unrecognized the backend resolves it to 'basicAuth' (pkg/models/auth_method.go:11-15). OAuth 2.0 works only with Jira Cloud — selecting it forces jsonData.hosting to 'cloud' (src/components/ConfigEditor.tsx:94-96).

To configure Basic Auth, set jsonData.authMethod='basicAuth', jsonData.url to your Jira base URL, jsonData.user to the account email, and put the API token in secureJsonData.token. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authMethod":"basicAuth","url":"https://mycompany.atlassian.net","user":"user@example.com"},"secureJsonData":{"token":"<your-jira-api-token>"}}. secureJsonData.token is required (pkg/models/settings.go:65-71). If jsonData.user is empty the backend sends the token as a Bearer token instead of HTTP Basic auth (pkg/plugin.go:253-264) — the pattern for a Jira Data Center personal access token.

To configure OAuth 2.0, set jsonData.authMethod='oauth2', jsonData.url, jsonData.oauthClientID and jsonData.cloudId, and put the client secret in secureJsonData.oauthClientSecret. Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"authMethod":"oauth2","url":"https://mycompany.atlassian.net","oauthClientID":"<your-oauth-client-id>","cloudId":"<your-jira-cloud-id>"},"secureJsonData":{"oauthClientSecret":"<your-oauth-client-secret>"}}. oauthClientID, oauthClientSecret and cloudId are all required (pkg/models/settings.go:56-64); the backend performs a client-credentials grant against https://auth.atlassian.com/oauth/token and targets https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/<cloudId> (pkg/plugin.go:214-244). OAuth 2.0 is Jira Cloud only.

Legacy interpretation: datasources created before jsonData.authMethod existed store no authMethod; the backend's GetAuthMethod resolves any empty or unknown value to 'basicAuth' (pkg/models/auth_method.go:11-15), so such instances authenticate with jsonData.user + secureJsonData.token. Do not assume a missing authMethod means the datasource is unconfigured.

secureJsonData values (token, oauthClientSecret) are write-only: they can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether a secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read the secureJsonFields object (e.g. secureJsonFields.token == true) instead of secureJsonData.

jsonData.url is required and is stored in jsonData (not the datasource root url); the backend prepends 'https://' when the value has no http(s):// scheme (pkg/models/settings.go:43-45), so 'company.atlassian.net' becomes 'https://company.atlassian.net'. jsonData.hosting selects the REST API version — 'cloud' uses /rest/api/3, 'server' (Jira Data Center / Jira Server) uses /rest/api/2 (pkg/plugin.go:177-180). For a non-scoped Basic Auth token the base URL is jsonData.url; for scoped tokens or OAuth 2.0 it is https://api.atlassian.com/ex/jira/<cloudId>, so jsonData.cloudId is required in those cases (pkg/plugin.go:214-228).
